consumer_electronics.title: Xbox is now XBOX
consumer_electronics.summary: Xbox just allcapsmaxxed: Meet XBOX. This isn't a joke; Microsoft appears to be actually rebranding Xbox to XBOX. Asha Sharma, Xbox CEO, ran a poll on X earlier this week, asking fans whether Microsoft should use Xbox or XBOX. The results were in favor of XBOX, and the company has now renamed its X account. […]

consumer_electronics.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
consumer_electronics.summary: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection program to all users over the age of 18 - meaning just about anyone can have the platform hunt for potential deepfakes of themselves. The likeness detection feature uses a selfie-style scan of a person's face to monitor YouTube for lookalikes. If there is a match, YouTube

consumer_electronics.title: Windows 11 tests an adjustable taskbar and resizable Start menu
consumer_electronics.summary: Microsoft's latest Windows 11 test will allow you to reposition the taskbar and change the size of the Start menu. The update, which is rolling out to Windows 11 Insiders in the Experimental channel, lets you place the taskbar on the bottom, top, left, or right side of the screen. consumer_electronics.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
consumer_electronics.summary: ArXiv, a popular platform for preprint academic research, is taking a new step to attempt to reduce the volume of papers that include AI slop. consumer_electronics.title: The Analogue 3D is finally getting save states
consumer_electronics.summary: Analogue has released a new firmware update for its Analogue 3D console that adds the Memories save state feature. consumer_electronics.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle
consumer_electronics.summary: OpenAI announced yet another reorganization Friday, consolidating certain areas and making company president Greg Brockman the official lead of all things product. consumer_electronics.title: One of Intel’s fastest desktop CPUs is $50 off

consumer_electronics.title: AI radio hosts demonstrate why AI can’t be trusted alone
consumer_electronics.summary: Andon Labs has been running a series of experiments in which AI agents run businesses without human intervention. Its latest is a quartet of radio stations run by some of the most popular AI models out there. consumer_electronics.title: Does Trump Mobile know how many stripes are on the American flag?

channel_news.title: NeeDohs are fetching a premium on resale sites
channel_news.summary: Eager NeeDoh collectors are flocking to resale sites as the toy goes viral. The stretchy toys, like Pop Mart's Labubus, are selling for a pretty penny on eBay and StockX, with some of them going for a premium of more than 400%.

channel_news.title: Marketplace Briefing: Why Amazon discontinued its AI-powered Rufus chatbot for Alexa shopping agent
channel_news.summary: Amazon replaced its Rufus chatbot with a new AI assistant called Alexa for Shopping this week as AI search becomes a bigger part of its online store.

channel_news.summary: Starbucks' new collaborations include the debut of a limited-edition weighted vest and a fitness challenge.

channel_news.title: Modern Retail+ Research: Marketers’ AI use rises, but tech skills stall
channel_news.summary: Modern Retail’s survey — which has been conducted annually since 2022 — found that marketers’ adoption of AI technology has risen significantly. In 2022, 44% of brand and agency pros said their companies were investing in AI technology. That percentage rose to 57% in 2023 and 71% in 2024, before hitting 86% in 2025.

channel_news.title: Unpacked: What Maryland’s dynamic pricing ban says about the electronic shelf label debate
channel_news.summary: Maryland passed the first U.S. law banning data-driven price hikes on groceries, but it stops short of restricting electronic shelf labels or frequent price changes.

channel_news.title: Heineken shares its marketing strategy for the summer of soccer as World Cup hype ramps up
